generated_parts.label: Dorsal Control Module x 2

verify.value: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles
Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals
PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns
All required features are present in the part_tree/geometry_ops
Estimated mass is 50g, within acceptable limits
All P0 features are traceable to geometry_ops
All interfaces are reciprocal and named with exact neighboring subsystem labels
Critical joints have visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent

risk.desc: Potential interference between the battery and the controller board due to proximity
risk.mit: Increase the clearance between the battery and the controller board

risk.desc: The snap fit interface with the hand back shell may not be robust enough
risk.mit: Consider adding additional locking mechanisms to the snap fit interface

risk.desc: The band clamp interface with the wrist band may not provide sufficient rotational stability
risk.mit: Evaluate the use of additional fasteners or a different interface type to improve stability

risk.desc: The connector mate interface with the connector mate module may not be secure enough
risk.mit: Ensure that the connector mate interface is properly secured with a locking mechanism

risk.desc: The heat sink may not be adequately dissipating heat from the controller board
risk.mit: Consider increasing the surface area of the heat sink or adding a fan for active cooling

physics_paths.description: Power from battery to controller board
Signal from controller to actuator
Heat from controller board to heat sink
Human access for service
Data from controller board to external device

Band clamp interface with wrist band
Connector mate interface with connector mate module

verification_focus.description: Ensure motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles
Ensure each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals

risk.desc: Wrist quick release mechanism may not be robust enough for frequent use.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the wrist quick release mechanism and use a stronger material.

risk.desc: Finger clearance gauges may not be precise enough, leading to discomfort or malfunction.
risk.mit: Use high-precision manufacturing processes to ensure the finger clearance gauges meet the required dimensions.

risk.desc: Actuator module plug may not provide sufficient strain relief, leading to wiring damage.
risk.mit: Design the actuator module plug with additional strain relief features and ensure proper routing of wires.

risk.desc: Emergency release pull tab may be difficult to access in an emergency situation.
risk.mit: Improve the design of the emergency release pull tab to ensure it is easily accessible and clearly marked.

risk.desc: Soft liner zone may not provide adequate comfort for extended use.
risk.mit: Select a softer and more comfortable material for the soft liner zone and conduct user testing to validate comfort.
